ConsultationRequest.title: ConsultationRequest
ConsultationRequest.description: Represents a user's request for expert manufacturing guidance and consultation services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ConsultationRequest entity.

name.description: The full name of the user requesting consultation.

phone.description: The phone number of the user for contact purposes.

email.description: The email address of the user for communication.

message.description: A detailed message from the user describing their consultation needs.

consultationOptions.description: A list of selected consultation options (e.g., Design Optimization, DFM Analysis).

requestDate.description: The date and time when the consultation request was submitted.

ConsultationFile.title: ConsultationFile
ConsultationFile.description: Represents an uploaded design file associated with a consultation request.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ConsultationFile entity.

consultationRequestId.description: Reference to the ConsultationRequest this file belongs to. (Relationship: ConsultationRequest 1:N ConsultationFile)

fileName.description: The original name of the uploaded design file.

fileType.description: The MIME type or file extension of the uploaded design file (e.g., 'application/step', 'image/jpeg').

fileUrl.description: The secure URL where the design file is stored in cloud storage.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the design file was uploaded.

definition.description: Stores individual requests for expert manufacturing consultation submitted via the public form. Accessible primarily by administrators.

params.name: consultationRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific consultation request.

definition.description: Stores references to design files uploaded as part of a specific consultation request. Accessible primarily by administrators, as a sub-resource of the parent request.

params.name: consultationRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent consultation request this file belongs to.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific consultation design file.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for consultation requests follows a simple hierarchical model, optimizing for administrative access and debuggability. All consultation requests are stored in a top-level collection `/consultationRequests/{consultationRequestId}`. Any design files uploaded as part of a request are stored in a subcollection `/consultationRequests/{consultationRequestId}/files/{fileId}`.

**Authorization Independence:** This structure achieves Authorization Independence because both `ConsultationRequest` and `ConsultationFile` entities are designed for admin-only access. There are no user-specific ownership or collaboration fields (like `ownerId` or `members` map) required for these entities based on the provided schema and user story. Authorization rules will directly check if `request.auth.uid` belongs to an administrator role (e.g., by checking a separate `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection). This avoids any `get()` operations on parent documents for authorization purposes within the `ConsultationFile` subcollection, as the parent's (ConsultationRequest's) authorization is implicitly handled by the admin role check at the top level. The documents themselves do not carry denormalized authorization context for multi-user access because it is not required for this admin-centric feature.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** This design fully supports Query-as-Permission (QAPs). For an administrator, listing documents in `/consultationRequests` will retrieve all consultation requests without the need for client-side filtering or complex rule logic. The security rule will simply verify `if isAdmin()` for `list` operations. Similarly, for `/consultationRequests/{consultationRequestId}/files`, an administrator can list all files associated with a specific request, again without filtering, by confirming `if isAdmin()`. The homogeneous security posture (admin-only) for both collections ensures that `list` operations are always QAP-compliant, returning all accessible documents without requiring additional filtering logic in the rules.
